note: Mathlib/GroupTheory/Coxeter/Matrix.lean:69 -- structure CoxeterMatrix (B : Type*) where ... symmetric matrix of naturals, diagonal 1, off-diagonal ≠ 1; file docstring cites Baez's 'Coxeter and Dynkin Diagrams' and states this is the standard alternate (matrix) encoding of the same data a Coxeter-Dynkin diagram carries (edge labels m_ij). Mathlib has the matrix encoding, not a graph/diagram structure -- related, not exact.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Matrix.lean:69 with exactly the claimed structure and Baez citation in the file docstring (verified against mathlib4 checkout); QID label 'Coxeter–Dynkin diagram' confirmed via Wikidata; 'related' (not 'exact') is honest since Mathlib formalizes the matrix encoding, not a diagram/graph object.

note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CliffordAlgebra/SpinGroup.lean:293 -- def spinGroup (Q : QuadraticForm R M) : Submonoid (CliffordAlgebra Q), defined as infimum of pinGroup and CliffordAlgebra.even. This formalizes the Spin group itself, not a representation of it (no spinor/half-spin representation construction found) -- nearby but a distinct object from 'spin representation'.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at SpinGroup.lean:293 as claimed (Submonoid, inf of pinGroup and even subalgebra -- the Spin group, not a representation); grepped mathlib4 for SpinRepresentation/spinRepresentation, none exists, so no closer formalization is being passed over; QID label/description ('representation of the spin groups') confirmed via Wikidata; match_kind already downgraded to 'related' with 'low' confidence and the note itself flags the group-vs-representation distinction -- honestly hedged, not overclaimed.

note: Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Cotangent.lean:299 -- abbrev CotangentSpace : Type _ := (maximalIdeal R).Cotangent, docstring 'The A ⧸ I-vector space I ⧸ I ^ 2' for R a local ring with maximal ideal I -- the algebraic/scheme-theoretic (m/m²) cotangent space at a point, which generalizes the differential-geometric dual-of-tangent-space definition to arbitrary local rings; Mathlib has no smooth-manifold CotangentSpace/CotangentBundle (only TangentSpace exists there).
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Cotangent.lean:299 with the exact abbrev/docstring quoted; confirmed Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold has TangentSpace (IsManifold/Basic.lean:1042) but grepped and found no CotangentSpace/CotangentBundle for manifolds, so the note's contrast holds; QID label/description ('dual space to the tangent space in differential geometry') confirmed via Wikidata; m/m² is the standard algebraic-geometry generalization of the manifold cotangent space -- 'generalization' is the correct match_kind, not 'exact'.
